title: plectranthus djalonensis
intro: Plectranthus djalonensis, also known as Camptacris, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. The leaves and stems of Plectranthus djalonensis have been traditionally used in West African folk medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant has been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the relief of pain and swelling. In herbal preparations, Plectranthus djalonensis is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically in the form of ointments or poultices.
